summary: Gruber shares a brief, playful post teasing readers about a hidden easter egg menu in BBEdit's About box while refusing to reveal how to access it. He also links to an interview with himself at Under the Iron. The post is characteristic of Gruber's affection for BBEdit and software craftsmanship details.
key_points: BBEdit contains a hidden easter egg menu in its About box
Gruber deliberately teases but refuses to reveal the easter egg, respecting the tradition of discovery
He links to an interview with himself at Under the Iron
money_quotes: No, I will not tell you how to get it. It's an easter egg!
OK, it's an easter egg *menu*. No more hints.
key_insight: Easter eggs are meant to be discovered, not revealed — the delight is in the hunt.
